key_insight: The paper gives computable two-sided a posteriori error certificates for PINNs solving ODEs by pairing localized lower bounds from strong monotonicity with localized upper bounds from one-sided Lipschitz conditions.
engineering_relevance: This enables more trustworthy PINN deployments by quantifying prediction uncertainty without needing the exact solution, especially for certified control and simulation workflows.
method: Derive lower error bounds on certified state-space domains using localized strong monotonicity.
Derive sharper upper bounds using localized one-sided Lipschitz constants and ODE residuals.
For linear systems, express certificates via eigenvalues of the symmetric part of the system matrix and add signed-residual finite-probe checks when initial conditions are enforced hard.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports closed-form and computable certificates for ODEs, including explicit linear-system formulas and a training-time regularizer based on the propagated upper bound.
watch_item: The certificates are only meaningful when the certified domain and monotonicity/Lipschitz assumptions can be verified; exact hard enforcement of initial conditions can make scalar lower bounds uninformative.
paper_title: Reliable Error Estimation for PINNs: Lower and Upper A Posteriori Bounds
